Traditional Goalkeeper.description: Focuses strictly on shot-stopping, positioning on the goal line, and claiming crosses inside the 6-yard box.
Traditional Goalkeeper.tactical_tendencies: Stays deep in the penalty area. Relies on long clearances when under pressure. Rarely participates in short-passing build-up.
Traditional Goalkeeper.formation_synergy: Best suited for low-block defensive setups (e.g., 5-4-1, 4-4-2 Flat) where defense sits deep.

Sweeper Keeper (SK).description: Acts as the 11th outfield player. Sweeps up balls behind a high defensive line and actively participates in possession recycling.
Sweeper Keeper (SK).tactical_tendencies: Positions high up near the edge of the box. Key to starting short-passing build-up from the back. Will rush out to clear long balls behind the CBs.
Sweeper Keeper (SK).formation_synergy: Essential for high-line possession styles (e.g., 4-3-3 Attacking, 3-4-3 Diamond) and Gegenpressing.

Ball-Playing Defender (BPD).description: A center-back tasked with initiating attacks and breaking opponent pressing lines with progressive passing or carrying.
Ball-Playing Defender (BPD).tactical_tendencies: Circulates possession comfortably. Plays vertical line-breaking ground passes to midfielders or long diagonal balls to wingers.
Ball-Playing Defender (BPD).formation_synergy: Synergizes with 4-3-3, 4-2-3-1, and 3-4-3 systems that build from the back.

Stopper Center-back.description: An aggressive, front-foot defender who steps out of the defensive line to engage attackers early.
Stopper Center-back.tactical_tendencies: Aggressively tackles, interrupts build-up, wins aerial duels early, and commits tactical fouls if bypassed.
Stopper Center-back.formation_synergy: Pairs well with a 'Cover' CB in 4-4-2 or 3-center-back systems (3-5-2, 3-4-3) where they can step out safely.

Cover Center-back.description: A reading-heavy defender who sweeps up loose balls and covers the space behind the Stopper CB.
Cover Center-back.tactical_tendencies: Sits slightly deeper, tracks runs behind the defense, avoids lunging into tackles, and marks spaces.
Cover Center-back.formation_synergy: Crucial partner to Stopper CBs in 4-4-2 or 4-2-3-1 setups.

Inverted Full-back (IFB).description: A fullback who tucks inside into midfield during possession to act as a temporary central midfielder/pivot.
Inverted Full-back (IFB).tactical_tendencies: Moves centrally to form a double pivot (e.g., 3-2 structure) in possession, protecting against central counters and overloading the midfield.
Inverted Full-back (IFB).formation_synergy: Used heavily in Pep-style 4-3-3 or 4-2-3-1 to build a 3-2-4-1 or 3-2-5 attacking shape.

Overlapping Full-back.description: A traditional attacking fullback who runs along the touchline outside the winger to deliver crosses.
Overlapping Full-back.tactical_tendencies: Runs past the winger to stretch the opposition defense, crossing from deep or from the goal-line. Recovers quickly to cover wide spaces.
Overlapping Full-back.formation_synergy: Synergizes with narrow wingers / Inside Forwards in a 4-3-3 or 4-2-3-1 system.

Attacking Wing-back (CWB).description: Operating in a 3-back system, covers the entire flank, acting as the primary source of wide defense and wide attack.
Attacking Wing-back (CWB).tactical_tendencies: Pushes very high in possession (acting as a winger). Requires high defensive work-rate to track back into a 5-man line.

Deep-Lying Playmaker (DLP/Regista).description: A creative midfielder operating deep in front of the defense, orchestrating the team's build-up and tempo.
Deep-Lying Playmaker (DLP/Regista).tactical_tendencies: Drops deep to receive from CBs. Looks for long switches of play or vertical passes. Shields space with positioning rather than physical tackling.
Deep-Lying Playmaker (DLP/Regista).formation_synergy: Fits as the single pivot in 4-3-3 or the deep playmaker in a 4-2-3-1/4-4-2 Diamond.

Anchor / Destroyer.description: A purely defensive midfielder focused on breaking up opposition play and shielding the center-backs.
Anchor / Destroyer.tactical_tendencies: Sits deep, maintains position, makes interceptions, and plays simple passes to creative teammates immediately upon recovery.
Anchor / Destroyer.formation_synergy: Crucial in 4-2-3-1 (as part of the double pivot), 4-3-3, and narrow diamond blocks.

Box-to-Box Midfielder (B2B).description: A dynamic midfielder who runs between both boxes, contributing heavily to both defensive press and final-third attacks.
Box-to-Box Midfielder (B2B).tactical_tendencies: Presses high, triggers counters, makes late runs into the opponent's penalty area, and tracks back to defend in own box.
Box-to-Box Midfielder (B2B).formation_synergy: Fits perfectly in flat 4-4-2, 4-3-3 (as a No. 8), and 4-2-3-1 (in double pivot).

Mezzala (Half-Space Attacker).description: An attacking central midfielder who drifts wide to exploit the spaces between the center and the flanks (half-spaces).
Mezzala (Half-Space Attacker).tactical_tendencies: Runs into the channels, combines with the winger and fullback, and shoots from the edge of the box.
Mezzala (Half-Space Attacker).formation_synergy: Essential role in modern 4-3-3 Attacking and 3-1-4-2 systems.

Carrilero (Shuttler).description: A lateral midfielder who covers the wide spaces left by attacking fullbacks, shifting from side to side.
Carrilero (Shuttler).tactical_tendencies: Maintains structural balance. Rarely makes vertical runs into the opponent box; focuses on lateral coverage and keeping possession.
Carrilero (Shuttler).formation_synergy: Crucial in narrow diamond formations (4-1-2-1-2) or asymmetric 3-5-2 blocks.

Shadow Striker (SS).description: An attacking midfielder who plays in the hole behind the striker, behaving like a second forward when entering the box.
Shadow Striker (SS).tactical_tendencies: Makes forward runs beyond the striker to catch defenders off guard. Exploits spaces created by a dropping target man/false nine.
Shadow Striker (SS).formation_synergy: Pairs extremely well with a False Nine or Target Man in 4-2-3-1, 4-4-1-1, or 4-2-2-2.

Inverted Winger / Inside Forward.description: A wide attacker playing on the opposite side of their stronger foot, cutting inside to shoot or combine centrally.
Inverted Winger / Inside Forward.tactical_tendencies: Drifts inside into half-spaces and the box. Leaves the touchline wide for overlapping fullbacks. Acts as a primary goal scorer.
Inverted Winger / Inside Forward.formation_synergy: Primary offensive force in 4-3-3 Attacking, 4-2-3-1, and 3-4-3 systems.

Classic Winger.description: A winger who hugs the touchline, beats their marker on the outside, and crosses the ball.
Classic Winger.tactical_tendencies: Maintains wide positioning to stretch defenses. Runs down the flank and delivers crosses to strikers.
Classic Winger.formation_synergy: Suits flat 4-4-2, 3-5-2 (as wingbacks), and traditional 4-5-1 setups.

False Nine (F9).description: A striker who drops deep into midfield to draw out opposing center-backs and act as a playmaker.
False Nine (F9).tactical_tendencies: Vacates the center-forward position, links up play with midfielders, and plays through-balls to inside-running wingers.

Target Man.description: A physically imposing striker who acts as the focal point for long passes, holding up play for runners.
Target Man.tactical_tendencies: Receives back-to-goal under physical pressure. Wins aerial duels, holds the ball to let midfielders join, and scores from close-range crosses.
Target Man.formation_synergy: Synergizes with long-ball styles, wing play, 4-4-2 Flat, and 3-5-2.

Advanced Forward / Poacher.description: A clinical striker who plays off the shoulder of the last defender, focusing entirely on finishing chances inside the box.
Advanced Forward / Poacher.tactical_tendencies: Stays on the edge of the offside line. Makes diagonal runs in behind. Rarely drops deep to help build-up; prioritizes scoring.
Advanced Forward / Poacher.formation_synergy: Commonly paired with a Target Man or creative No. 10 in 4-4-2, 3-5-2, and 4-2-3-1.

Pressing Forward.description: A defensive-minded forward tasked with leading the team's high-pressing line.
Pressing Forward.tactical_tendencies: Aggressively chases opposing center-backs and goalkeeper. Blocks passing lanes, forces turnovers, and tracks back when needed.
Pressing Forward.formation_synergy: Essential in high-pressing systems like Gegenpressing, 4-2-2-2, and modern 4-3-3.

midfield_matching.3_vs_2_midfield: A 3-midfielder formation (like 4-3-3 or 4-1-2-1-2) will naturally overload a 2-midfielder block (like 4-4-2 Flat). To counter this, one of the 4-4-2 strikers must drop deep as a defensive target, or wide midfielders must tuck inside.
midfield_matching.double_pivot_neutralization: A 4-2-3-1 double pivot sits deep enough to neutralize late runs from 4-3-3 Mezzalas, but can get overrun if the 4-3-3 wingers tuck inside to create a central 4v2 overload.

wing_space_exploitation.back_three_flank_vulnerability: Formations with 3 at the back (e.g., 3-5-2, 3-4-3) leave space behind their wing-backs. High, pacey wingers (e.g., in a 4-3-3) can isolate the wide center-backs in 1v1 situations if the wing-backs fail to recover quickly.

low_block_unlocking.half_space_overloads: To break down a low-block 5-4-1, teams utilize Mezzalas and Inverted Wingers to create numerical overloads in the half-spaces (the channels between CBs and wing-backs), dragging defenders out of position.
